items.title: Always write ℓ(θ) before optimizing
items.avoid: Plugging data into a black-box loss without deriving Σ log P(xᵢ | θ).
items.prefer: Expand ℓ(θ) explicitly, confirm sign, then minimize −ℓ or set ∇ℓ = 0.
items.why: Explicit ℓ catches wrong distributions, missing constants, and sign errors that look like bad hyperparameters.
items.example: Bernoulli: ℓ(p) = Σ[yᵢ log p + (1−yᵢ) log(1−p)]; derivative w.r.t. p sets p̂ = ȳ.

items.title: Check parameter constraints
items.avoid: Reporting interior critical points that violate p ∈ [0,1], σ > 0, or λ > 0.
items.prefer: Optimize over the valid domain (bounds, reparameterization like log σ) and compare boundary values.
items.why: MLE is constrained optimization; invalid θ make L(θ) undefined or zero.
items.example: Exponential λ must be positive; use λ = exp(η) or scipy bounds [(1e-6, None)].

items.title: Match loss to assumed noise model
items.avoid: Defaulting to MSE or cross-entropy without stating the generative model.
items.prefer: Document P(y | x, θ) and show training loss = −ℓ(θ) under that model.
items.why: MLE is only principled when the likelihood family matches the data-generating process.
items.example: Heavy-tailed residuals: consider Student-t NLL instead of Gaussian MSE.

items.title: Use log-sum form in code
items.avoid: Computing ∏ P(xᵢ | θ) in a loop for large n.
items.prefer: Accumulate `log_prob` per example; report total ℓ and average NLL per example.
items.why: Numerical stability and compatibility with autograd (PyTorch `log_softmax`, `nll_loss`).
items.example: `loss = -log_probs[range(n), y].mean()` instead of multiplying softmax probabilities.
